result.stdout:
Universoid.Spatial.Esri.ArcPyCli.Console.App 1.0.8
Universoid

  run            Run the Command in the ESRI arcpy enabled python environment. 
                 Example Usage: arcpy-cli <options> --pycmd [option] ... [-c 
                 cmd | -m mod | file | -] [arg] ...

  list           Retrieve a list of all available ESRI arcpy enabled python 
                 environments.

  set-current    Set the ESRI ArcGIS X64 python environment.

  help           Display more information on a specific command.

  version        Display version information.

Parsing failed...

result.stdout:
Universoid.Spatial.Esri.ArcPyCli.Console.App 1.0.8
Universoid

  -f, --format          (Default: console) Switch to 'json' to get the cli 
                        output into a json/yaml readable format for further 
                        processing.

  -e, --environment     Switch between the Python environments, if available. 
                        (Python 2.7 only 'current' environment available)

  -a, --architecture    (Default: Unknown) Switch between 'X32' and 'X64' 
                        process architecture.

  -r, --runtime         (Default: Unknown) Switch between 'ArcMap' and 'ArcGIS'
                        runtime.

  --path                Choose this option to get only the Python path on the 
                        command line. Use this for Scripts, etc.

  -v, --verbose         (Default: false) Show verbose logging information on 
                        the console.

  --help                Display this help screen.

  --version             Display version information.

Parsing failed...

result.stdout:
Universoid.Spatial.Esri.ArcPyCli.Console.App 1.0.8
Universoid

  -e, --environment     (Default: current) Switch between the Python 
                        environments, if available. (Python 2.7 only 'current' 
                        environment available)

  -a, --architecture    (Default: X64) Switch between 'X32' and 'X64' process 
                        architecture.

  -r, --runtime         (Default: ArcGis) Switch between 'ArcMap' and 'ArcGIS' 
                        runtime.

  -f, --format          (Default: console) Switch to 'json' to get the cli 
                        output into a json/yaml readable format for further 
                        processing.

  --pycmd               Use this parameter to separate the arcpy cli options 
                        from the native python command. The software will just 
                        split the command here.

  -v, --verbose         (Default: false) Show verbose logging information on 
                        the console.

  --help                Display this help screen.

  --version             Display version information.

Parsing failed...

staticCommands.name: list
staticCommands.description: Retrieve a list of all available ESRI arcpy enabled python environments.

options.description: Switch between the Python environments, if available. (Python 2.7 only 'current' environment available)
Switch to 'json' to get the cli output into a json/yaml readable format for further processing.
Choose this option to get only the Python path on the command line. Use this for Scripts, etc.
Show verbose logging information on the console.

staticCommands.name: run
staticCommands.description: Run the Command in the ESRI arcpy enabled python environment. Example Usage: arcpy-cli <options> --pycmd [option] ... [-c cmd | -m mod | file | -] [arg] ...

options.description: Switch between the Python environments, if available. (Python 2.7 only 'current' environment available)
Switch to 'json' to get the cli output into a json/yaml readable format for further processing.
Use this parameter to separate the arcpy cli options from the native python command. The software will just split the command here.
Show verbose logging information on the console.

staticCommands.name: set-current
staticCommands.description: Set the ESRI ArcGIS X64 python environment.

options.description: The name of the new environment.
Show verbose logging information on the console.
